data.title: Whale Sells ASTEROID Tokens for Significant Profit
data.description: A whale has reportedly sold 2.79 billion ASTEROID tokens, according to Odaily. The transaction resulted in an exchange for 503 ETH, valued at $1.17 million. This sale comes just five days after the whale purchased the tokens for only $575, achieving a return of over 2000 times the initial investment.

data.title: A whale (a cryptocurrency investor) posted a screenshot showing its holdings of 717.491 BTC, with a floating profit of $1.226 million, after setting "10 major goals" in advance.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a whale who posted a screenshot showing a position of 717.491 BTC with a floating profit of $1.226 million, opened at $75,731.7 and is currently trading at $77,439.7.

data.title: A whale spent 1.9 ETH to buy 44.03 billion FLORK in 2023, which is now worth over $350,000.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale spent 1.9 ETH (US$3,941) to buy 44.03 billion FLORK on April 18, 2023, which is now worth more than US$350,000. However, the whale's wallet address has not been active for 976 days.

data.title: Japanese Yen Faces Limited Movement Amid Economic Concerns
data.description: The Japanese yen remains confined within the 158.00-160.00 range against the U.S. dollar, according to Jin10. The potential for Japanese intervention in the foreign exchange market is curbing upward movement, while uncertainties in the Middle East, high energy prices, and demand for the dollar as a safe haven are limiting downward movement. A significant rebound in the yen could occur only if the Bank of Japan raises interest rates, potentially pushing it to around 155 or stronger. If the Bank of Japan unexpectedly acts during its policy meeting on April 27-28, it could significantly suppress the dollar-yen exchange rate, possibly causing a decline before Japan's upcoming 'Golden Week' holiday. However, this scenario seems unlikely at present. Data from Totan Research/ICAP indicates that the probability of a rate hike next week has decreased to 9%, while the likelihood of a June hike has risen to 62%. Despite concerns from the Bank of Japan and the Ministry of Finance about the inflationary impact of a weak yen, the market perceives that Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda and most policy committee members remain cautious about the economic impact of the Middle East situation amid heightened worries about economic growth setbacks.

data.title: Aave continues to experience capital outflows, with total deposits falling to approximately $30.7 billion.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, funds are still flowing out of Aave, with $15.1 billion flowing out in just three and a half days. Total deposits have dropped from $48.5 billion before the Kelp security incident to $30.7 billion now, representing a loss of roughly one-third. The situation for two other major lending platforms is as follows: 1. Morpho: Total deposits have dropped from $11.7 billion before the rsETH incident to $10.2 billion now, a net outflow of $1.5 billion. 2. Spark: SparkLend's TVL has increased from $1.9 billion before the rsETH incident to $3.2 billion now, a net inflow of $1.3 billion. Spark has absorbed some funds withdrawn from Aave by whales/institutions (such as Justin Sun and the whale who bought in with $500 million in February).

data.title: AI TRENDS | Baidu Launches Orion AI Engine at Industry Conference
data.description: On April 22, Baidu announced the launch of its 'Nebula Plan' at the Baidu Alliance Conference, opening its Orion AI engine to the entire industry. According to Jin10, the MCP service will share revenue with developers, allowing manufacturers to directly integrate industry-specific modules. Leading smartphone manufacturers such as Xiaomi, Honor, and Vivo have already joined the initiative. Baidu's Orion is an AI search full-chain universal service solution open to partners, leveraging the Wenxin Assistant. Through Master Agent and multiple Skills orchestration, it can access Baidu's search capabilities and services in e-commerce, travel, health, and more, creating a closed loop from information acquisition to task delivery.

data.title: Japan's Bond Prices Drop Amid Middle East Tensions and Rising Oil Prices
data.description: On April 22, Japan's government bond prices experienced a significant decline following two consecutive days of gains. According to Jin10, this downturn was driven by uncertainties surrounding peace negotiations between the U.S. and Iran, coupled with rising oil prices, which have reignited inflation concerns. The yield on Japan's 10-year government bonds increased by approximately 2 basis points to 2.4%, while the 30-year bond yield rose by about 1.5 basis points to 3.570%. Katsutoshi Inadome, a senior strategist at Sumitomo Mitsui Trust Asset Management, commented that the rise in yields is primarily fueled by the persistent high oil prices due to the volatile situation in the Middle East.

data.title: SuiLend Confirms Platform Operations Unaffected by Security Incident
data.description: SuiLend has announced that all platform functions, including deposits, lending, withdrawals, and repayments, are operating normally. According to Odaily, the company assured users that their funds remain secure. The team is closely monitoring the developments of the recent Volo Protocol security incident and will continue to provide updates.

data.title: Lufthansa Cuts 20,000 Short-Haul Flights Amid Rising Fuel Costs
data.description: Lufthansa has announced plans to eliminate 20,000 short-haul flights from its European summer schedule in response to soaring jet fuel prices. Bloomberg posted on X that the airline's decision comes as fuel costs have doubled since the onset of the Iran war. This move is part of Lufthansa's strategy to mitigate the financial impact of increased operational expenses. The airline aims to optimize its flight operations by focusing on more profitable routes and reducing less economical services. The adjustments are expected to help Lufthansa manage its resources more efficiently during the challenging economic climate.

data.title: Artist Alex Schaefer Connects Protest Art to Bitcoin's Origins Ahead of 2026 Exhibition
data.description: Artist Alex Schaefer recently shared insights into his Banks on Fire protest paintings, which will be featured in a Bitcoin 2026 exhibition in Las Vegas from April 27 to 29. According to NS3.AI, Schaefer's artwork draws inspiration from the public outrage following the 2008 bank bailouts and aligns with Bitcoin's foundational narrative as a response to financial system failures. The exhibition aims to highlight the intersection of art and cryptocurrency, emphasizing Bitcoin's role as a symbol of resistance against traditional banking practices.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 640 USDT with a 1.83% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 22, 2026, 02:55 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 640 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 640.109985 USDT, with a narrowed 1.83% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: SBF's early $200,000 investment in Cursor's funding round would currently be worth approximately $3 billion if it did not sell its stake.
data.description: SpaceX announced today that it has reached a partnership agreement with AI programming startup Cursor, which has granted SpaceX the option to acquire Cursor for $60 billion later this year, or to pay $10 billion to advance the collaboration. Cursor's developer, Anysphere, reportedly completed a $400,000 pre-seed funding round in April 2022, co-invested by Alameda Research and Heroic Ventures. Alameda invested $200,000 in Anysphere, acquiring approximately 5% of the shares. This stake was sold at its original price during FTX's bankruptcy liquidation; if it hadn't been sold, it would currently be worth approximately $3 billion.

data.title: The Reserve Bank of Australia is closely monitoring Anthropic's AI model.
data.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) is closely monitoring Anthropic's newly launched "Mythos" artificial intelligence model. Anthropic claims the model is powerful enough to handle sophisticated cyberattacks. In a statement, the RBA said, "We are working with peer regulators, governments, and regulated entities. The RBA will continue to assess the impact of these technological advancements to ensure the continued security and stability of the financial system." Meanwhile, regulators around the world are discussing with financial institutions how to address the cybersecurity risks associated with "Mythos." (Jinshi)

data.title: Suilend responds to Volo security incident: Platform operating normally, user funds safe.
data.description: SuiLend stated in a post on the X platform that all platform functions are currently operating normally, including deposits, loans, withdrawals, and repayments, and user funds have not been affected. Meanwhile, the team is closely monitoring the progress of the previous Volo Protocol security incident and will continue to release updates.

data.title: The Base standalone network upgrade, "Base Azul," is scheduled to launch on the mainnet on May 13th.
data.description: Base has announced that its independent network upgrade, "Base Azul," will launch on the mainnet on May 13, 2026. Azul is already live on the testnet and aims to improve security, performance, and the developer experience. Key updates include: 1. Activating a multi-proof system (combining TEE and ZK proofs) to advance Stage 2 decentralization and enable withdrawals to be completed in as little as one day; 2. Integrating into a single high-performance client stack, base-reth-node and base-consensus, reducing the number of empty blocks from approximately 200 per day to approximately 2, and maintaining burst throughput of 5000 TPS on multiple occasions; 3. Aligning with the Ethereum Osaka execution layer specification.

data.title: Ethena Resumes Cross-Chain Operations for sUSDe and USDe on LayerZero
data.description: Ethena has announced the resumption of cross-chain operations for sUSDe and USDe on LayerZero. According to Foresight News, the platform has reactivated these services across all chains. Ethena has increased the DVN configuration from 2/2 to 4/4 and maintained the existing hourly rate limit of $10 million to enhance security measures.

data.title: MAGA's market capitalization surpasses $20 million, with a daily increase of over 260%.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the market capitalization of MAGA, the Meme coin on the SOL chain, has surged past $20 million, currently trading at $21 million, representing a daily increase of over 260%. The token is described as a community-driven Meme token built around the narrative of "alien disclosures." As countries begin to disclose information about UFOs, UAPs, and extraterrestrial contact, global interest in extraterrestrial life continues to rise. The platform aims to aggregate content related to alien disclosures, UFO sightings, and related cultural topics. Odaily reminds users that the price of Meme coins is highly volatile; investors should exercise caution.

data.title: Crypto Liquidations Surpass $60 Million in One Hour
data.description: According to NS3.AI, recent data from Coinglass reveals that cryptocurrency liquidations have exceeded $60.08 million in the past hour. Notably, short positions made up $59.37 million of this total, highlighting a significant impact on traders betting against the market.

data.title: North Korean Hacker Group Lazarus Releases New macOS Malware Targeting Cryptocurrency
data.description: PANews posted on X (formerly Twitter) that the North Korean hacker group, Lazarus, has launched a new macOS malware toolkit named 'Mach-O Man.' This toolkit is designed to target cryptocurrency and high-value executives. The alert was issued by 23pds, the Chief Information Security Officer at SlowMist, warning users to be cautious of potential risks associated with this new threat.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Declines Amid Middle East Uncertainty
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump's inconsistent statements regarding a ceasefire and potential military action have led to market unease, according to BlockBeats. This uncertainty has resulted in a decline across all major U.S. stock indices at the close of trading, as investors remain concerned about the situation in the Middle East.

While some believe Trump's approach reflects a degree of deliberation and consideration, these traits are not typically associated with him, as he is often viewed as impulsive and reactive. The market is now awaiting a response from Iran.
